result.title: Reflect and normalize extracted metadata (validate and convert star count, standardize license)

details.intent: After obtaining raw page-extracted values, validate presence of each field, normalize numeric formats, and prepare values for saving.
target_checks: Confirm all target_facts were captured from the visit node; if any missing, mark as missing and list which ones.
Normalize stars_approximate: convert shorthand (e.g., '44.7k') to approximate integer representation for clarity (store both raw and normalized forms).

Standardize license_type: if license text is e.g. 'Python-2.0' or 'PSF License', note exact text and, if possible, map to an SPDX identifier string in a secondary field (but do not fetch external sources).

transformation_rules.stars_normalization: If value contains 'k' or 'K', multiply numeric portion by 1000; if contains ',' remove commas and parse. Provide both raw_star_string and stars_estimate_int.
transformation_rules.license_standardization: Keep license_raw as extracted. If license_raw contains well-known tokens (e.g., 'PSF', 'Python-2.0', 'MIT'), set license_spdx_hint to that token; otherwise leave null.

measurable_output_fields.stars_estimate_int: integer (approximation) or null if cannot parse
measurable_output_fields.fields_present: list of target_facts captured vs missing

verification_steps: Compare presence of DOM elements captured in visit results against extraction_plan to ensure no field was omitted.
If normalization fails (e.g., unexpected star string format), record the failure reason and preserve raw text for audit.
details.reasoning: Normalization and validation are separated to preserve raw evidence from the visit node. This step reduces uncertainty by making numeric and license values explicit and machine-friendly before saving.

2e4830c8-d679-4de3-aabb-0b6e67564ac5.title: Visit https://github.com/python/cpython and extract repo metadata

details.intent: Directly visit the CPython GitHub repository page (mandated) and extract concrete metadata visible on the page.
details.extraction_plan: On the repository main page, locate: (a) the language chip/summary (usually top-right of file list / under repo name) for primary programming language; (b) the Stars button/element showing star count (capture raw string like '123k' or '123,456'); (c) the license element (often a 'License' link/badge under repo description or in repository metadata) and, if present, the exact license name/spdx id; (d) the short description shown under the repository name. Copy the exact text and any numeric strings as seen on the page. Record the exact page URL visited as source_url. Save candidate link references found on the page (e.g., LICENSE file link) into the links collection per visit tool behavior.

measurable_output_fields.stars_approximate: string (raw from page, e.g., '44.7k' or '110k+'; will be normalized later)
measurable_output_fields.license_type: string (exact text as shown, e.g., 'PSF License' or 'Python-2.0')
measurable_output_fields.brief_description: string (1-2 sentence description from repo header)

verification_steps: Ensure the visit returned the repository main HTML page (status 200) and the expected DOM elements exist (repo title, description, stars element, language chip, license link).
If a license link to a LICENSE file is present in page links, include that link in saved results (but do not fetch it in this node).
Capture raw strings rather than converting – normalization/conversion performed in the following node so evidence is preserved.
details.reasoning: This mandatory direct-visit step is required by the user. It gathers primary evidence (page text and numerical strings) needed to answer the four requested facts. Visiting the exact URL satisfies the constraint 'must visit the GitHub page directly - do not rely on search results.' Note: Chroma/vector DB will be auto-queried by the system for link discovery as per platform behavior.
